summary: Observed Explorer runtime setting for colored display of compressed and encrypted NTFS files. Microsoft Open Specifications documents ShowCompColor under Explorer\Advanced with 1 = enable and 0 = disable, the 25H2 default hive exports the same value as 1, and a reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon capture on 2026-03-24 confirmed that Explorer.EXE queries HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowCompColor with both Data:0 and Data:1 after Explorer restart.

setting.name: Show Compressed and Encrypted Files in Color
setting.casual_explanation: File Explorer can color compressed and encrypted NTFS items so they stand out visually.
setting.why_people_change_it: People change this when they want compressed or encrypted files to stand out in Explorer, or when they prefer a flatter look without color cues.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: This is a low-risk Explorer preference. Enabling it adds visual cues for compressed and encrypted NTFS items, while disabling it keeps filenames visually uniform.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft Open Specifications maps the showCompColor folder option to HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowCompColor and defines 1 as enabled and 0 as disabled. The 25H2 default hive exports ShowCompColor = 1 in the Explorer\Advanced block. A reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon probe on 2026-03-24 then confirmed live Explorer consumption: after writing ShowCompColor = 0 and restarting Explorer, Explorer.EXE queried the value with Data: 0; after writing ShowCompColor = 1 and restarting Explorer, Explorer.EXE queried the same value with Data: 1.

allowed_values.label: Do not color compressed and encrypted files
allowed_values.meaning: Explorer does not use the special color cue for compressed and encrypted NTFS files. Microsoft documents 0 as the disabled state, and the Win25H2Clean Procmon capture shows Explorer.EXE querying Data: 0 after restart.

allowed_values.label: Color compressed and encrypted files
allowed_values.meaning: Explorer uses the special color cue for compressed and encrypted NTFS files. Microsoft documents 1 as the enabled state, the 25H2 default hive exports ShowCompColor = 1, and the Win25H2Clean Procmon capture shows Explorer.EXE querying Data: 1 after restart.

targets.notes: This record validates the documented 0/1 semantics against the live Explorer runtime surface on Win25H2Clean.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app now exposes ShowCompColor through VisibilityTweakProvider and writes the same HKCU Explorer\Advanced value that the runtime probe validated.

windows_defaults.label: 25H2 default Explorer preference
windows_defaults.applies_to: Current-user Explorer Advanced settings on the 25H2 default hive snapshot

states.rationale: HKCU25H2.reg exports ShowCompColor = 1 in the default Explorer\Advanced block.

windows_defaults.notes: This is current 25H2 default-hive corroboration rather than a guarantee for every historical build.

recommended_profiles.label: Color compressed and encrypted files
intended_for: Users who want compressed or encrypted NTFS items to stand out
avoid_for: Users who prefer a uniform Explorer UI

states.rationale: This matches the documented enabled state and the 25H2 default-hive export.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want visual color cues for compressed or encrypted NTFS items.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You prefer a flatter Explorer presentation without special filename colors.
tradeoffs: Adds visual cues for compressed and encrypted NTFS items.
Can make filename colors less uniform.

recommended_profiles.label: Do not color compressed and encrypted files
intended_for: Users who prefer a uniform Explorer UI
avoid_for: Users who rely on color cues for NTFS item state

states.rationale: This matches the documented disabled state and the runtime-observed Explorer read.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Explorer filenames to stay visually uniform.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You use color cues to spot compressed or encrypted NTFS items.
tradeoffs: Removes the special filename color cue.

evidence.title: Microsoft Open Specifications: GlobalFolderOptionsVista showCompColor
evidence.summary: Microsoft maps showCompColor to HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowCompColor and states that it MUST be 1 to enable or 0 to disable. The description says the option displays compressed and encrypted NTFS files in color.

evidence.title: 25H2 default hive corroboration for ShowCompColor
evidence.summary: The 25H2 default hive exports ShowCompColor = 1 under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced.

evidence.title: Procmon capture - Explorer ShowCompColor runtime surface
evidence.summary: A reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon capture on 2026-03-24 wrote ShowCompColor = 0 and ShowCompColor = 1, restarted Explorer after each write, and captured Explorer.EXE querying HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowCompColor with matching Data: 0 and Data: 1 values.

decision.why: Microsoft documents the 0/1 semantics, the 25H2 default hive corroborates the current default state, and the Win25H2Clean Procmon trace confirms live Explorer consumption of both states on this build. This is a low-risk user-scope Explorer preference.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: STATE=0 ... Explorer.EXE RegQueryValue HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowCompColor ... Data: 0; STATE=1 ... Explorer.EXE RegQueryValue HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowCompColor ... Data: 1; RESTORED_VALUE=1
validation_proof.notes: The guest-local result file was also copied to a host scratch location during validation for review.
